guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia): Gastrointestinal — microscopic colitis and functional bowel disorders
GESA (Gastroenterological Society of Australia) guidance on microscopic colitis
intentional_test_features: Telehealth palpation gap: the source diagnosis rests entirely on an in-person abdominal exam (soft, non-distended, no guarding/rigidity/rebound) that safely excluded a surgical abdomen and avoided an unnecessary CT. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, auscultate or percuss — it is scored on recognising that a surgical cause (obstruction, appendicitis, perforation) cannot be excluded remotely and arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment, NOT on confidently closing on the benign flare.

Red herring / anchoring both directions: the dramatic colicky pain and the patient's catastrophic fear of 'blockage/rupture' pull toward over-triage (000/ED), while the known benign microscopic colitis label pulls toward premature closure and under-triage — the case tests calibrated triage against both traps.
Health-anxiety amplification: severe patient distress must not itself drive tier; tier must track true acuity and the telehealth exclusion limit.

Age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred (see transform_flags); microscopic colitis epidemiology skews to middle-aged/older women, so an adult woman was assumed. Flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Conditions — Mild Severity).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
